headline.time: Your estimates land closest in the {{label}}.

detail.time: About {{betterAccuracy}}% accurate in the {{betterLabel}}, vs {{worseAccuracy}}% in the {{worseLabel}}.

accuracyCorrelationsLocked.headline: Know your sharpest hours.
accuracyCorrelationsLocked.sub: Some hours you read time better than others. See exactly when — and when to leave a little more buffer.

placeholder.title: Meet your time personality
placeholder.body: Answer a few quick questions, or keep logging and I'll figure it out.

biggestSurprise.detail: You guessed {{estimateMin}} min and it took {{actualMin}}. Worth a mental note for next time.

readiness.none: Your areas are still settling. A few more logs and the numbers sharpen.
readiness.one: One area reads honest now. The rest are catching up.
readiness.all: Every area reads honest now. Your numbers are yours.
readiness.some: {{count}} of your areas read honest now.

contextCorrelations.headline: Your {{value}} sessions land closest.
contextCorrelations.detail: About {{bestAccuracy}}% accurate on {{bestValue}} sessions, vs {{worstAccuracy}}% on {{worstValue}} ones.
contextCorrelations.meta: Based on the {{noun}} you noted across {{count}} sessions · learned on-device

contextCorrelationsLocked.headline: Find what moves your accuracy.
contextCorrelationsLocked.sub: When you note your energy after a session, that's a clue. Pro reads it back: whether low-energy days throw your estimates off.

driftNote.body: <bold>{{categoryName}}</bold> {{lead}} — it used to run {{early}}×, now nearer {{recent}}×. Your honest numbers already follow along.

locked.teaserWithContrast: We found your sharpest stretch — {{contrast}}× above your slump.

patternsEmpty.title: Your patterns are on the way
patternsEmpty.body: Time a few tasks and this is where you'll meet your time personality, your sharpest category, and the surprises worth noticing.

headline.planWins: Running the timer keeps you sharper.
headline.gutWins: You read time fine without the timer.

supporting.planWins: Timed tasks land {{timedPct}}% accurate vs {{retroPct}}% when logged after.
supporting.gutWins: Logged-after tasks land {{retroPct}}% accurate vs {{timedPct}}% when timed. Honestly — your gut does well here.

planExperiment.note_one: Based on {{timedCount}} timed and {{retroCount}} logged-after task.
planExperiment.note_other: Based on {{timedCount}} timed and {{retroCount}} logged-after tasks.

pending.body: A few more timed and a few logged-after tasks and you'll see whether the timer makes you sharper. No rush.

predictionCard.note_one: From your last {{count}} task, for a 15-min guess.
predictionCard.note_other: From your last {{count}} tasks, for a 15-min guess.

context.up: Your guesses and actual time are aligning.

stealsYourTime.headline: Where {{category}}'s extra minutes go.
stealsYourTime.meta: Based on {{sampleCount}} of {{totalOver}} times you noted why · learned on-device

stealsYourTimeLocked.headline: See where your time really goes.
stealsYourTimeLocked.sub: Every time you note why a task ran long, that's a clue. Pro reads them back: the cause behind your overruns, by category.

summary.improving: That's {{delta}} points sharper than when you started. Nice work.
summary.steady: Right where you started — and steady reads are a real skill.

whyNarrative.early: You start sharp and fade after lunch
whyNarrative.midday: You hit your stride around midday
whyNarrative.afternoon: Mornings warm up slow — you peak after lunch
whyNarrative.evening: You're a slow burn — you peak in the evening

steadyReader.title: The Steady Reader
steadyReader.blurb: Your guesses land close to reality. Quietly rare.

gentleOptimist.title: The Gentle Optimist
gentleOptimist.blurb: You lean hopeful, then mostly catch up. A little padding does it.

sprintOptimist.title: The Sprint Optimist
sprintOptimist.blurb: Your mind moves fast; the doing takes a touch longer. Now you know by how much.

dreamer.title: The Dreamer
dreamer.blurb: Big plans, generous timelines. Your honest numbers keep them grounded.

ripening.title: Your honest range
ripening.sub: The range Whenbee will show you — once it has seen enough of your timing to mean something.
ripening.footer: Each task you log quietly sharpens the range. Nothing to keep up with.

reveal.headline: Your range just got honest.
reveal.sub: Whenbee has seen enough of your timing. The Pro features below now have real data to work with — not guesses.
